hard.next_step: 50% confidence 2nd Jul- Proposal for Zycus has been taken to owner of MMC group- Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary and received a positive response and level of interest. Datuk Nural is now working channels to confirm budget tolerance for all 7 ports. Likely 1-2 weeks for responses (including Northport)- the sense is MMC will look at current proposal we have lodged ($240k at Northport) and factor/optimize across all- including small ports and average $150k per port x 7- this remains to be confirmed Met with Theta Edge - Group CEO on 11th and discussed the proposal in detail 6th Jun Proposal to Owner being planned- likely week of 8th 1st Jun- opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary) 6th April- Path to close- Agliaan (IT & PM for project) must present 2 prices (in total 3) to new CEO who only needs to see they did some diligence, given it is not going to tender- this is fair albeit a step we did not know about. We are coaching him on the format for comparison to be presented today or tomorrow for final sign off. It remains likely they can sign MSA to a 30th Mar date as there is additional financial benefit (we are offering added 1% per additional Port they bring us into and we close in future- potential upside additional 7 Ports) monitoring this account every 2-4 hourly 30th Mar- Revised proposal required to split implementation into 2 phases- Northport team was away on Hari Raya break and back today. Proposal has limited concessions tied to LOI 31st Mar- and full BOM signed by 7t April- 17th March - Proposal Submitted for S2P 12th Mar Final session complete- internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO) and ck to follow up our champion for close path. 11th Feb - have shared the workflow for the S2P they would like to see, plan to have this workshop in the last week of Feb after CNY 9th Feb-Scope back to S2P- management for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped- meeting on Wed 11th to discuss detail- would like sample contracts to eval. 29th Jan Siti now has approval to move forward with Zycus modules of choice- although mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires 20th Jan- Siti advises we are tracking to get management buy in- Ramco pushing them hard (are aware to P2P side for them at risk) 14th Jan- next step is pricing 5th Jan - Siti (our champion) has asked for costing estimates to present to CFO for (S2C) option and S2P option- in her exco meeting 23rd Jan Ramco have cancelled last 2 meetings and have put their P2P renewal at risk- this is a good opportunity for Zycus to move in, and dominate the MMC group of Ports in Malaysia- ck to drive value prop and aiming for a Q4 result 10th Dec Great f2f with Siti and team. They are strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco. They will bring proposal to management (CEO) who is interested in the procurement manual processes- and Tendering/sourcing 26th Nov Good meeting with Zalekha (Siti) who is keen to see Zycus demo in person. Open to replacing Ramco (Zalekha) to send current tender process flow . Zycus to prepare a customized demo covering tender, RFQ, CLM, and Teams. Zycus to include PTP case study and Ramco integration details. Both teams to propose a date for onsite demo (in coming 2–3 weeks).

forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the honest category, and the deal team itself cut the forecast from Best Case to Pipeline on 6 Jun 2026 when the deal re-scoped. The exposed element is the 30 Aug 2026 close date, not the category: it assumes group budget tolerance (responses due around 16 Jul 2026), commercial alignment across 7 ports, and a not-yet-re-established paper process all complete inside 7 weeks with no direct economic-buyer engagement yet. This is a date problem on an otherwise real deal, and the date will likely slip.
forecast_read.math: 52 days to close (30 Aug 2026 vs 9 Jul 2026 sweep). Stage is Formal Evaluation since the 6 Jun 2026 re-scope (amount 240k to 650k, stage reset from Shortlisted, forecast cut to Pipeline). The original 31 Mar 2026 LOI and 7 Apr 2026 full-BOM targets lapsed unsigned, and the close date has slipped twice (30 Mar to 30 Jun on 28 Apr 2026, then to 30 Aug on 1 Jun 2026). Budget-tolerance responses promised in 1-2 weeks from 2 Jul land around 16 Jul, leaving under 7 weeks for a 7-port group negotiation and contracting.

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain rather than a quantified business case: on the 25 Nov 2025 call, Nazual Azma Reffin said 'I think it's quite urgent now since we're supposed to migrate,' citing manual tender handling as the bottleneck. Internally, Zycus discussed a ~US$50k-per-port target price (Avoma, 30 Dec 2025), and the 2 Jul 2026 Next Step frames the group play at roughly $150k per port across 7 ports, but that is rep-side pricing calibration; no ROI or savings figure has ever been validated with the buyer. Quantifying the value case for the group-wide consolidation, tied to the budget-tolerance discussion now underway, is the key open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is now named at the highest level: Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, owner of the MMC group, who per the 2 Jul 2026 Next Step received the group-wide Zycus proposal with 'a positive response and level of interest.' The same log names 'Datuk Nural' as working internal channels to confirm budget tolerance across all 7 ports; that name resolves to no Salesforce contact role and stays unverified. Neither figure has ever appeared on a Zycus-attended call; every signal from that layer is relayed through the partner channel, so their authority and true sentiment remain unverified by Zycus directly. Securing a direct or partner-brokered session with the owner's office is the critical next step.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 11 Feb 2026 call the buyer set the evaluation lens: the full procurement flow 'from the MR, part number question... up to the billing side' with finance automation ('I want automation in payment'), a supplier portal, and a guided hands-on workshop of 90-120 minutes across the end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite with the AI add-ons. Ramco integration is an explicit criterion, including a reference customer 'that uses Zycus and also integrated with the Ramco system.' The 25 Nov 2025 call added the tender and e-bidding depth test: building the tender from documents, controlling bidding rounds and participating vendors, award through to PO or letter of award, and tender-fee deposit handling.

decision_process.narrative: The process has escalated through three tiers, visible in the call and Next Step sequence: (1) Northport working-level (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, Nazual Azma Reffin, Agilaan Praemkumar) drove discovery, demos and the proposal through Mar 2026; (2) Northport's 'new CEO' required Agilaan to present 2-3 price comparisons for sign-off around 6 Apr 2026 (outcome never logged); (3) as of 1 Jun 2026 the opportunity was merged into a group-wide MMC consolidation, taken to the group CEO (met 11 Jun 2026, logged as 'Met with the relevant stakeholder - Group CEO') and then to the MMC group owner (2 Jul 2026), with a group-level channel now confirming budget tolerance across all 7 ports, responses expected around 16 Jul 2026. This multi-tier chain has moved decision authority progressively upward and away from the original Northport contacts.

paper_process.narrative: A concrete paper process existed briefly in Mar 2026: the 17 Mar 2026 S2P proposal, a phased-implementation revision on 27-30 Mar 2026, an LOI target of 31 Mar 2026, and a full BOM/MSA signature target of 7 Apr 2026 (with a 1% incremental benefit offered per additional port brought in later). That target lapsed with no signature. Since the deal moved to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026, no contracting mechanics (MSA scope, legal review, procurement channel) have been re-established for the 7-port structure. Confirming what paper process applies once budget tolerance clears is unaddressed.

identify_pain.narrative: Northport's core pain is a manual, non-digitized tender and e-bidding process sitting alongside Ramco, which handles purchasing, supplier registration and renewals but has no e-bidding or tender capability. On the 25 Nov 2025 call the buyer said a prior Ramco-era e-bidding collaboration 'never materialized' and asked Zycus directly to propose: 'we want to see what you can provide to us.' A specific operational gap was also raised: tender-fee deposits are paid and receipted manually and would need a custom payment integration. Pain owner: Northport's tender and procurement team.

champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) was a strong, active champion through Q1 2026: per Next Step, on 10 Dec 2025 she and her team were 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco,' and on 29 Jan 2026 she secured management approval to move forward with Zycus modules. She was last referenced driving the deal in the 12 Mar 2026 entry ('follow up our champion for close path'), 17 weeks ago. Since the deal escalated to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026 she has not appeared in any update or call, and the current activity runs through the group CEO and the MMC owner's office instead. Her continued advocacy and access at group level is unconfirmed, and she is at risk of being bypassed.
sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (10 Dec 2025, 29 Jan 2026, 12 Mar 2026 entries)

competition.narrative: Ramco is the entrenched incumbent for P2P and purchasing across the MMC ports and has actively fought to keep the account: Next Step records it 'pushing them hard' on 20 Jan 2026, and Northport's management flip-flopped between retaining Ramco (29 Jan 2026) and dropping it (9 Feb 2026), with no confirmed resolution since. Competitors__c also lists Ivalua and SAP Ariba, but no call, Next Step, or task names either being actively evaluated by Northport or MMC; they carry no corroborating buyer evidence. The wedge against Ramco is e-bidding and tender capability plus AI-based contract authoring and scoring, which Ramco does not offer, backed by low-risk integration to the Ramco system of record.
sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (20 Jan, 29 Jan, 9 Feb 2026 entries)

critical_signals.text: The evaluation has reached the very top of the buying group: MMC group owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary reviewed the Zycus proposal on 2 Jul 2026 and responded with interest, after the group CEO discussed it in detail on 11 Jun 2026.
The deal is now a 7-port group play framed at roughly $150k per port against the $240k lodged at Northport, and group budget tolerance is unconfirmed, with responses expected around 16 Jul 2026.

No direct two-way buyer exchange has been verified since Agilaan Praemkumar acknowledged the proposal on 30 Mar 2026; the mid-April follow-ups on proposal value and client reference visits drew no logged reply.

Ramco remains the incumbent purchase-to-pay system across the MMC ports and fought hard to keep the account through January 2026; Northport's 9 Feb 2026 decision to drop it followed a flip-flop and has not been re-confirmed since.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Formal Evaluation deal that has expanded from a single-port ~$240k proposal into a 7-port MMC group consolidation worth roughly $1M, with a positive but rep-reported signal from the group owner, while the buyer side has had no direct two-way call or email with Zycus since 30 Mar 2026 and group budget confirmation and direct economic-buyer engagement remain outstanding.

win_position.text: A Zycus-authored proposal reached MMC group owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary and, per the 2 Jul 2026 Next Step, received 'a positive response and level of interest,' real interest at the top of the buying hierarchy.

The deal is genuinely multi-threaded at Northport: tender (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus), procurement (Nazual Azma Reffin), digital transformation (Agilaan Praemkumar) and corporate services (Azman Mohamed) all engaged through the 9 Dec 2025 onsite demo, the 11 Feb 2026 suite deep-dive and the March 2026 proposal round, and management approval to proceed with Zycus was logged on 29 Jan 2026.
No Zycus-attended call or email has directly reached the MMC group owner, the group budget channel, or Northport's new CEO; every signal from that layer is partner-relayed and rep-logged.

Champion Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus has not been referenced in the deal record since 12 Mar 2026, and the decision has moved above her to group level with no confirmed advocate inside the owner's office.

deal_momentum.text: The process climbed two buyer tiers in five weeks: the group CEO discussed the proposal in detail on 11 Jun 2026 and the group owner reviewed it on 2 Jul 2026, with budget-tolerance responses promised within 1-2 weeks (around 16 Jul 2026).

The 6 Jun 2026 re-scope (amount 240k to 650k, stage reset from Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation, forecast cut from Best Case to Pipeline) restarted the evaluation clock across 7 ports, and the close date has already slipped twice (28 Apr and 1 Jun 2026).
The last verified buyer action is Agilaan Praemkumar's 30 Mar 2026 proposal acknowledgement; the 10 Apr and 16 Apr 2026 follow-up emails show no logged reply.

customer_commitment.text: Northport asked for client reference visits to hear customer experience first-hand (11 Feb 2026 call), a buyer-initiated validation step Zycus followed up on 16 Apr 2026 and can still convert.
The owner's positive response is relayed through the partner channel and logged by the rep; no MMC group stakeholder has joined a Zycus call, replied in writing, or committed a dated next meeting.

deal_risk.text: Group budget tolerance across all 7 MMC ports is unconfirmed as of 2 Jul 2026, and the $650k booking rests on an unvalidated ~$150k-per-port frame that the rep himself logs as 'remains to be confirmed.'
Ramco is the entrenched incumbent P2P across the MMC ports, was 'pushing them hard' on 20 Jan 2026, and the 9 Feb 2026 drop-Ramco decision was itself a flip-flop never re-confirmed.
52 days remain to the 30 Aug 2026 close with the deal in Formal Evaluation, the March 2026 LOI/MSA plan lapsed, and no paper process re-established for the 7-port structure.

customer_preference.evidence: The only in-window preference signal is partner-relayed and rep-logged: the MMC group owner's 'positive response and level of interest' to the Zycus proposal (SF Next Step, 2 Jul 2026). The last buyer-voiced preference is stale: 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (10 Dec 2025 F2F log); no buyer call since 11 Feb 2026 restates preference.

business_case.evidence: Pain is confirmed (manual tender and e-bidding, urgency voiced by Nazual Azma Reffin on 25 Nov 2025) and a priced phased proposal is lodged (17-27 Mar 2026), but no quantified ROI or savings case has been validated with the buyer, and the ~$150k-per-port group frame (2 Jul 2026) is unconfirmed.

deal_movement.summary: The deal ran a full Northport-level cycle (discovery Nov 2025, F2F demo 9 Dec 2025, suite deep-dive 11 Feb 2026, workshop and proposal build through Mar 2026) that stalled at a 31 Mar/7 Apr 2026 LOI/MSA target. From 1 Jun 2026 the opportunity was re-scoped as a 7-port MMC group consolidation and routed through the local partner channel to the group CEO (11 Jun 2026) and ultimately the MMC group owner (2 Jul 2026), reportedly with a positive response. No Zycus-verified buyer call or reply has occurred since 30 Mar 2026.

items.change: Revised 2-phase proposal issued, tied to a 31 Mar 2026 LOI target; buyer acknowledged receipt 30 Mar
Close date pushed from 30 Mar to 30 Jun 2026
Opportunity reframed as a group-wide MMC 7-port consolidation; close date moved to 30 Aug 2026
Re-scope booked: amount 240k to 650k, stage reset to Formal Evaluation, forecast cut to Pipeline
Group CEO met to discuss the proposal in detail
Proposal presented to the MMC group owner; positive response and 7-port budget-tolerance check logged

day_summary.overall: The consolidated 7-port proposal reached the owner of the MMC group, Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, and drew a positive response and stated interest. Budget-tolerance soundings started across all 7 ports the same day, with responses expected in one to two weeks, and the working frame is that MMC will take the $240k Northport proposal and optimize it to roughly $150k per port across seven ports. It is the first movement at owner level, though it reached Zycus through the partner channel rather than a direct buyer conversation.

items.name: Group owner reviews the 7-port proposal
items.summary: MMC group owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary received the Zycus group proposal and responded with interest; budget-tolerance confirmation began across all 7 ports with answers expected by around 16 Jul.

competitive_position.summary: Ramco is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent P2P/ERP across the MMC ports, actively pushed back against replacement through January 2026, and Northport's stance on retaining vs dropping it flip-flopped as recently as 9 Feb 2026 with no confirmed resolution since. No new competitor evidence has surfaced in the last 90 days; the practical alternative if group budget does not clear is do-nothing, meaning these ports stay on Ramco P2P plus manual tendering. Ivalua and SAP Ariba sit on the Competitors__c field but appear in no call, Next Step, or task evidence found this sweep; treat them as unconfirmed field-only entries pending corroboration.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Northport's stated requirements (e-bidding and tender digitization, CLM, Teams-based intake, Ramco integration, AI-based scoring) map cleanly to the products in scope (iContract, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRisk, Merlin Intake); the open fit questions are group-wide standardization across 7 ports and completing the requested reference validation.

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers matter most now: protect the original champion as the deal escalates, re-verify the Ramco displacement decision before the group commits, and multi-thread beyond the single relayed channel before the group-wide budget decision lands.
flags: No quantified value case exists for a $650k-$1M group decision: pain is qualitative (manual tendering, no e-bidding) with no ROI, savings, or cycle-time figure ever validated; co-author a value case with Northport's team and finance to arm the owner-level conversation.

The group-level play runs through a single relayed channel while champion Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (unmentioned since 12 Mar 2026) and primary decision maker Nazual Azma Reffin have no recent logged touch; re-thread Northport and open contacts at the other 6 ports before the budget decision lands.

Re-verify the Ramco displacement decision before any group-wide commitment: Ramco was 'pushing them hard' in Jan 2026, management flip-flopped on retaining it (29 Jan vs 9 Feb 2026), and nothing since confirms the incumbent is out.

What is missing: any mention of or contact with her since the 12 Mar 2026 'follow up our champion for close path' entry, and any evidence she has a voice in the group-level conversation now running through the MMC owner's office.

expansion_context.note: No sibling opportunity on this account is Closed-Won, but PTP (Port of Tanjung Pelepas), a sister port in the same MMC group, is a long-standing Zycus customer and is the buyer's own requested reference anchor (Avoma 25 Nov 2025 and 11 Feb 2026 calls).

scope_change.to: 7-port MMC group consolidation booked at $650k, framed at roughly $150k per port (~$1.05M) pending budget tolerance
scope_change.detail: On 1 Jun 2026 the rep logged that the opportunity 'will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner'; on 6 Jun 2026 the amount moved 240k to 650k, the stage reset from Shortlisted to Formal Evaluation, and the forecast was cut from Best Case to Pipeline. The group frame ($150k x 7) 'remains to be confirmed' per the 2 Jul 2026 Next Step.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The AI story landed early but has not been revisited in the group frame: the buyer's IT contingent probed Merlin's Teams-based intake on 25 Nov 2025 ('How do you incorporate the procurement intake... inside Microsoft team?'), Merlin Intake is in the product scope, and the 30 Dec 2025 internal readout reported 'they did see Anna. They loved Anna, but they kept saying budget,' so ANA was held as a priced option. No AI-specific conversation has been logged since March 2026, leaving the AI differentiation unexercised in the owner-level pitch.

ai_fit_signal.summary: The buyer engaged genuinely with the AI layer (detailed Merlin/Teams intake questions on 25 Nov 2025, a strong reaction to ANA per the 30 Dec 2025 internal readout) but consistently gated it on budget, and no AI conversation has been logged since the deal escalated to the group frame.

items.detail: Group-wide budget tolerance across all 7 MMC ports is unconfirmed; the $650k booking (and the ~$150k x 7 = ~$1.05M frame) 'remains to be confirmed,' and budget pressure has shaped the deal since scoping (ANA held as optional on budget concerns, 30 Dec 2025).
items.source: SF Next_Step__c (2 Jul 2026); Avoma 30 Dec 2025 internal prep call

contributions.detail: Economic buyer never directly engaged — the proposal reportedly reached owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary via internal channels, but no Zycus-attended meeting with him, Datuk Nural, or the new Group CEO is logged (the '6 Jun meeting with Theta Edge Group CEO' isn't corroborated in Tasks/Avoma). ► Get a direct Zycus session with the group owner or CFO before this advances further.
Deal dark 105 days — last real meeting 26 Mar 2026; zero sessions in the 30/60/90-day windows and nothing scheduled forward.

Real qualification depth banked — decision process, decision criteria, and pain are all 'confirmed' in MEDDPICC, built on 8 meetings (6 with transcript) since Nov 2025, including a 16-min demo (9 Mar 2026) and a dedicated S2P workshop.

Champion has gone quiet — Siti Zalekha, who said she was 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (10 Dec 2025) and secured internal approval (29 Jan 2026), hasn't surfaced since her March follow-up ask. ► Re-engage Siti directly to confirm she still holds sway at group level.
Incumbent threat unresolved — Northport flip-flopped on dropping Ramco between 29 Jan and 9 Feb 2026, with no resolution logged since.
Two signature deadlines already missed — the 31 Mar LOI and 7 Apr full-BOM targets both lapsed unsigned, and the close date has moved out to 30 Aug 2026.
105 days since any buyer meeting — last logged session was the 26 Mar 2026 Working Lunch; count_30d/60d/90d all show zero.
No future meeting booked — next_scheduled is null and has_future_calendar is false.
Close date pushed repeatedly — slipped from the original ~30 Mar 2026 sign target through 28 Apr and 1 Jun to the current 30 Aug 2026.
Next step still dated and active — the 2 Jul entry says the proposal reached MMC owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary with a 'positive response,' and Datuk Nural is working budget channels.
Cadence looks rep-driven, not buyer-driven — rep notes 'monitoring this account every 2-4 hourly,' but no Completed Task or Avoma record shows the buyer initiating since March.
Rep's own confidence is 50/50 — the latest next-step entry (2 Jul) opens with '50% confidence,' signaling real uncertainty on timing.

Buyer put in real evaluation work — a dedicated S2P workshop, sample-contract review, and a formal demo (9 Mar 2026, 16 min) show genuine internal investment.
Budget tolerance still unconfirmed — both the $650k Northport figure and the ~$1.05M group-wide frame 'remain to be confirmed' per the rep's own 2 Jul note.
Decision authority moved out of reach — the deal shifted from a Northport committee (where Zycus was preferred) up to the MMC group owner level, where Zycus has no direct relationship.

Group-wide budget unconfirmed — the entire ~$1.05M multi-port frame hinges on budget tolerance still being worked through channels per the 2 Jul note.

cro_panel.header: NORTHPORT (MALAYSIA) BHD — Formal Evaluation · $650K · closes 30 Jul
cro_panel.intro: A Formal Evaluation deal that has expanded from a single-port ~$240k proposal into a 7-port MMC group consolidation worth roughly $1M, with a positive but rep-reported signal from the group owner, while the buyer side has had no direct two-way call or email with Zycus since 30 Mar 2026 and group budget confirmation and direct economic-buyer engagement remain outstanding

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Ramco is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent P2P/ERP across the MMC ports, actively pushed back against replacement through January 2026, and Northport's stance on retaining vs dropping it flip-flopped as recently as 9…

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Obtain written confirmation of the 7-port budget-tolerance decision through the partner channel by 16 Jul. (by 16 Jun)
Book the client reference visit Northport requested, leading with PTP as the same-group peer. (by 23 Jun)
Re-engage champion Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus to confirm her role in the group-level decision. (by 21 Jun)

gaps: The 9 Mar 2026 demo transcript captured only the AV setup; the workshop content itself is unread (outcome taken from the 12 Mar 2026 Next Step: 'Final session complete')
The MMC group owner and the group budget channel have never been on a Zycus-attended call; that layer is known only through the rep's dated Next Step log (knowledge gap)
'Datuk Nural' (2 Jul 2026 Next Step) resolves to no Salesforce contact role or Avoma attendee; identity unverified
No confirmation of the outcome of Agilaan Praemkumar's April 2026 price-comparison presentation to Northport's new CEO
